title: Structural Slanted Trendline Breakouts with 3-Swing Validation
author: Christian Benjamin (lynnchris)
verdict: HIGH EXTRACT — 3-swing validation is stronger than our 2-touch trendlines. ThirdSwingToleranceATR=0.15 prevents false trendlines. Anti-repainting deduplication via bar timestamp. Directly upgrades StructureEngine v14.0 _fit_trendline().

algorithm.step_2_anchor_pair: First 2 swings (A, B) define the line slope and y-intercept
algorithm.step_3_third_swing_validation: Third swing C is valid if |C.price - LinePriceAtTime(A,B,C.time)| <= ThirdSwingToleranceATR * ATR
algorithm.step_4_breakout: crossedUp = prev_close <= line_prev + buf AND curr_close > line_curr + buf
algorithm.step_5_dedup: lastBuyBarTime = bar timestamp; only one signal per bar per direction

MinSwingSize.description: Absolute or ATR-relative minimum swing height

ThirdSwingToleranceATR.description: Third swing proximity to line (in ATR units)

BreakoutBufferPoints.description: Points beyond line for breakout trigger

BreakoutUseClose.description: Use close price (not high/low) for breakout

RequireThirdSwingAfterAnchor2.description: Third swing must occur after second anchor

upgrade_vs_v14.upgrade: Require 3rd swing confirmation before using trendline for gate H
upgrade_vs_v14.formula: tol = atr * ThirdSwingToleranceATR; valid = |C.price - interpolated_y| <= tol

keep: CrossedOver/CrossedUnder pattern from Part 39 reused here
improve: Add n_touches >= 3 requirement to _fit_trendline before gate H uses the line
ThirdSwingToleranceATR=0.15 maps to our tol = atr * 0.15 (tighter than our current 0.5)
Dedup: track last_tl_break_bar_idx — no repeat gate H violations within 5 bars
